mentions.note: Hosting Monday 6pm analyst meeting on cancer portfolio post-ASCO 2026; cancer dominance challenged by J&J—wait for details before recommending.
Reported really good quarter but sold off on day when pharma/healthcare was disliked; CEO Rob Davis did good job; stock up $3, maneuvering back to $120; press the button.
Trading at 13x earnings; healthcare rotation ongoing. Cramer suggests waiting for pullback, 'divide by 10' to view as $11 stock potentially at $10.50.
Down 1.3%; pharma weakness signals inflation concerns overriding growth optimism; rates/inflation backdrop weighing on sector.
39% rally since end-Oct; intact uptrend, 3–8% pullbacks ideal entries. Keytruda pillar; just bought cancer-drug biotech. Chart bullish, money flow strong. Analyst sees $130 by year-end.
Classic healthcare dividend payer and buyback machine; solid earnings and reasonable valuation vs. tech; benefiting from healthcare rotation.

Transformation beyond Keytruda: $70B commercial opportunity (up $20B YoY), 10 key growth drivers, diverse pipeline (enlisticide, Wincreased, flu antiviral); superior pharma getting rewarded with P/E expansion.
Real wins from bountiful acquisition spree; has a lot to talk about; stock a little too heavy on Crude but can spread out the wealth.
Pharma defensive play; can weather weak economy; recommended alongside Colgate and J&J as hedge.
